center.title: Cleanup Center
center.subtitle: Policy-driven, explainable, reversible reclamation of library storage.
center.howItWorks: Cleanup proposes; people decide. A run turns policy matches into candidates — nothing is removed. A candidate becomes an action only inside an approved plan whose fingerprints still match the world, and every removal lands in Trash or Quarantine, both recoverable.

tile.policiesDesc: Versioned cleanup policies and their conditions
tile.runsDesc: Scans and the candidates they produced
tile.plansDesc: Review and approve what a run proposed
tile.protectionsDesc: What automatic cleanup may never touch

policies.title: Cleanup Policies
policies.subtitle: A policy is a draft plus frozen published versions. Publishing does not enable it — arming a destructive policy is a separate, deliberate act.
policies.emptyDesc: Start from a template or create an empty policy, then publish and enable it when you are ready.

draft.title: Policy document (draft)
draft.help: The policy is a JSON document evaluated by the platform's constrained engine — there is no code and nothing executable. A full visual condition builder is planned; for now, edit the document directly.

policies.confirmDelete: Delete this policy and all its versions? This cannot be undone.
policies.confirmEnable: Enable this policy? An enabled, published policy can be run by a schedule or trigger.
policies.publishFirst: Publish the policy before running it.

runs.title: Cleanup Runs
runs.subtitle: A run walks a policy's scope and records what it found. It produces candidates, never deletions.
runs.emptyDesc: Run or simulate a policy to see candidates here.

plans.title: Plans & Approvals
plans.subtitle: A plan pins the policy version and each candidate's fingerprint, so what you approve is exactly what runs. Approval grants permission to act; execution acts, re-checking everything first.
plans.emptyDesc: Build a plan from a run's candidates to see it here.
plans.confirmApprove: Approve this plan? It grants permission to remove the listed files to {{destination}}.
plans.confirmExecute: Execute this plan now? Each file is re-checked immediately before it is touched; anything protected, locked, busy or changed is skipped.
plans.rejectReason: Why are you rejecting this plan?

quarantine.title: Quarantine
quarantine.subtitle: Held files, moved out of the library but never deleted. Restore puts a file back exactly where it came from; purge is the one irreversible step.
quarantine.emptyDesc: Files a cleanup plan quarantined will appear here until restored or purged.
quarantine.confirmRestore: Restore this file to its original location?
quarantine.confirmPurge: Permanently delete this file? This bypasses Trash and cannot be undone.
quarantine.overwrite: Overwrite whatever is now at the original path

protections.title: Protections
protections.subtitle: A protection tells automatic cleanup it may never touch something. Conditional protections fail closed; a legal hold cannot be lifted by an ordinary operator.
protections.emptyDesc: Protect a file, library, show, tag or path so cleanup can never remove it.
protections.confirmRevoke: Revoke this protection? Cleanup will be free to act on the target again.
protections.revokeReason: Why are you revoking this protection?

create.title: New protection
create.reasonPlaceholder: Why this exists — required for the audit trail

oldUnwatchedLowRes.name: Old, unwatched, low-resolution movies
oldUnwatchedLowRes.desc: Movies released before 2001, never watched, below 1080p.
oldUnwatchedLowRes.rationale: Old low-res films nobody has played are the safest first thing to reclaim. Approval required.

lowUse10bit.name: Low-use 10-bit media
lowUse10bit.rationale: Report only — 10-bit is usually the better encode, so this flags for review rather than removal.

supersededH264.name: Superseded 8-bit H.264 with a verified replacement
supersededH264.desc: 8-bit H.264 files where an equal-or-better copy already exists.
supersededH264.rationale: Quarantine, approval required — only ever with a verified surviving replacement.
